rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Attorney must give notice of the Amended petition to all required parties on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120 and include the remote appearance information that is required by the 10th amended general probate order found on the court's website. Notice cannot be given prior to the petition being filed and it appears the notice filed 2/22/2022 does not include the accurate title of the filed petition. 2) Please explain the starting date for the account. Page 1 does not state. Page 2 line 8 alleges it starts October 31, 2015. Page 5 line 6 alleges it begins November 1, 2015. The GC-400 alleges the account begins November 1, 2015. Property on Hand at start of account period alleges it begins October 31, 2015. 3) Please explain and provide the legal basis for the judicial council form GC-400 property on hand at beginning of account not matching the prior account property on hand at the end of the account. 4) Please explain and provide the legal basis for why First Rep. Account #2699 the ending balances of the prior account does not match the starting balances of this account. 5) Please explain the legal costs reimbursement for 5/26/20216. It appears $670.80 was paid to the attorney but receipts show only $573.30 in expenses. Attorney Vicken Sonentz appeared via courtcall for the 5/10/2016 hearing. Please explain why the estate should pay the costs of a roundtrip ticket and hotel reservation for the attorney when the attorney appeared via courtcall. 6) Please explain the legal costs reimbursed for 10/21/2016 and file the missing receipts. $200.21 was alleged for lodging no receipt filed and counsel appeared via court call. Please explain why the estate should pay the cost of lodging when the attorney appeared via courtcall. It is alleged the court call was $116.00 but the provided court call confirmation page which is not a receipt alleged the statewide fee for court call appears is $86.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MYRIAM W. OLIVEN U/D MAY 29,2009 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the legal basis for the account beginning 10/23/2020. Franza Giffen was confirmed as successor trustee on 08/12/2015 and there have been no prior accounts in this case. 2) Please explain the legal basis for attorney fees request beginning 06/19/20219. 3) Please explain the legal basis for the property on hand at beginning of the account being $1,402,227.17. The I&A's filed appear to total $1,470,230.17. 4) Please file a bank statement that shows the $100,000.00 transfer to conservatorship of Myriam W. Oliven. 5) Please explain and file a receipt for Dixie Miner - Care Management through 7/12/21. Notice of Conservatee's death alleges Conservatee died 06/26/2021. 6) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] 7) Please file trustee timesheets that comply with LRSF 14.52. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES KAVANAGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Administrator Carmel Kavanagh To Approve Creditors Claim Of Administrator
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The guardian ad litem must also appear. 1) The decedent and petitioner signed the Memorandum of Agreement ("MOA") providing for the decedent's child/spousal support obligations and division of property. At the time of the decedent's death, the marital status had not been terminated by the family court's judgment. The MOA provides that: "This Memorandum contains all the essential terms of settlement and shall be independently enforceable under CCP section 664.6; this Memorandum is not protected by the mediation or settlement conference privilege. However, the parties intend and agree that a Marital Settlement Agreement ("MSA") containing these and other standard terms will be prepared and signed by them." Family Code section 3580. 2) The MOA provides for guideline monthly child support to be calculated on an annual basis by assuming the decedent's "non-modifiable" minimum earned income of $150,000 per year. The petition requests continuing child support after the decedent's death. 3) The MOA provides that the $1,000,000 spousal support is "non-modifiable". Family Code sections 3591(c) & 4337. 4) The petition requests "immediate" payments of certain claims and annual payments of other claims that are contingent or are not due. Probate Code sections 11420, 11422(b), 11461 & 11467. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: LOUIS MARK BUELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Verified Petition For Declaratory Judgement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The revised petition alleges that: "This is a petition seeking to enforce California statutes which require third parties to honor a power of attorney", one of the authorized purposes of a proceeding concerning a power of attorney under Probate Code section 4541(f), although the revised petition relies on CCP section 1060 applicable to declaratory relief. The deceased principal was a resident of Marin County. The petitioner/attorney-in-fact also resides in Marin County. Under section 4523, the proper county for commencement of a power of attorney proceeding must be determined in the following order of priority: (a) county of principal's residence; and (b) county of attorney-in-fact's residence. 2) According to the attorney's affidavit, a petition was filed in Marin County for an order transferring the deceased principal/settlor's assets to his trust, before this petition was filed in San Francisco County. The attorney objects to providing information about the petition filed in Marin County, stating that such information does not affect venue and has no legal relevance. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH KIRN BROZELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUDY FRANCES ELLMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/13/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please file Proof of Publication. (Prob. C. 8120, et seq). 2) Please file new waivers of account on mandatory judicial form DE-142 that are dated. 3) A family tree that traces Decedent to those listed on page 4 #8 may be helpful to the court. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C. 3) The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)]. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE 147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
